Exp11 MODEL SELECTION: mT5-base (580M, general multilingual), full data x3ep. Val mean 0.322 ~= AfriTeVa-base 3ep (0.324) and < AfriTeVa warm5_long (0.356), DESPITE 35% more params. Key finding: African-specific pretraining (AfriTeVa) beats a larger general model on these languages. NOT submitted (ROUGE below AfriTeVa).
Exp12 warm-start from warm5_long +2ep = 9 total epochs. Val mean 0.361 (+0.005 vs warm5_long 0.356). Monotonic but DIMINISHING returns (+0.024,+0.008,+0.005). Recombined: combined_retrieval_warm9.csv
NEW BEST. Public 0.4706=0.37*0.4838+0.37*0.4045+0.26*0.5457. warm9 LLM-judge 0.5457 (up from 0.5335). 9 epochs; diminishing returns but still +0.003 public.

Exp13 BALANCED SAMPLING: warm-start from warm9, oversample all subsets to 4000 each (+2ep). Val mean 0.369->0.385 (+0.016, biggest jump since warm5). NUANCED: helped Swa+0.05/EngUga+0.05/EngGha+0.03/Aka+0.005 but HURT Amh-0.026/Lug-0.013 (oversampling-by-duplication overfits the smallest, most distinct sets). COMPETITION CLOSED (rank 399). Private 0.4626 vs public 0.4742 (gap 0.012). DETAIL: LLM-judge IDENTICAL 0.5597 both splits (model generalized perfectly); ROUGE cols dropped R1 0.484->0.469, RL 0.405->0.388 (retrieval baseline more brittle).
